negativePrompts.note: Flux 2 does not support negative prompts. Use semantic negatives in the main prompt instead, e.g. 'without text' rather than a negative prompt.

strengths: Photorealistic rendering with fine detail
Strong prompt adherence for complex scenes
Excellent text rendering in images
tips: Use detailed, natural-language scene descriptions rather than keyword lists
Specify lighting, camera angle, and atmosphere explicitly
Include art style references when aiming for non-photorealistic output
Describe spatial relationships between subjects clearly
Mention material textures and surface qualities for realism
avoid: Keyword-style tag lists separated by commas
Negative prompt syntax or embedded negatives
Overly short prompts with minimal detail

tips: Start with the main subject, then layer in environment and mood
Specify aspect ratio intent in the description if composition matters
Use cinematic or photographic framing terms for dynamic compositions
Describe the desired changes relative to the source image
Focus on what should change rather than re-describing the entire scene
Mention style transfer goals explicitly if changing artistic direction

negativePrompts.note: Flux Schnell does not support negative prompts. Use positive rephrasing in the main prompt, e.g. 'sharp focus' instead of 'no blur'.

strengths: Very fast generation with good quality
Natural language understanding from the Flux family
Solid prompt adherence for straightforward scenes
tips: Write concise natural-language descriptions focused on the main subject
Keep prompts shorter than Flux 2 Pro since Schnell is optimized for speed
Specify the most important visual elements first as token priority matters
Use camera and lens references for photographic quality
avoid: Keyword-style comma-separated tag lists
Overly complex multi-subject scenes that need Pro/Max capacity
Quality tags like '8k masterpiece' which waste token attention

negativePrompts.note: No separate negative prompt parameter. Embed semantic negatives in the main prompt, e.g. 'no extra fingers, avoid watermarks'.

strengths: Flash-speed generation at professional quality
Web-search grounding for current real-world data
Strong text rendering in 10+ languages
Supports up to 14 reference images for style transfer
tips: Write detailed natural-language descriptions; more detail yields closer results
Use photography terminology like camera types and lighting setups
Leverage reference images for complex style transfer and edits
Specify depth and spatial layering for realistic scene composition
avoid: Keyword-only prompts without descriptive context
Relying on a separate negative prompt field that does not exist
Very short prompts that produce generic output

tips: Front-load the subject and action, then add environment and style
Reference specific camera models like GoPro or Fujifilm for photographic style
Include lighting setup details such as 'three-point softbox' for studio looks
Describe the target changes clearly: background swap, color shift, or style change
Combine multiple reference images when blending styles or elements
Mention which aspects of the source image to preserve

negativePrompts.note: GPT Image does not support negative prompts. Rephrase positively, e.g. 'empty scene' instead of 'no people'.

strengths: Near-perfect text rendering in images
Precise prompt following with 10-20 objects
Conversational iteration within a session
tips: Structure prompts as: background/scene, subject, key details, constraints, intended use
Put text in quotes or ALL CAPS and add 'EXACT, verbatim' for accurate rendering
Name the intended use like 'for a billboard ad' to set quality mode
Add explicit light sources to prevent overly dark output
Repeat exact style wording across prompts for consistency
avoid: Omitting light source descriptions which causes dark images
Changing style phrasing between iterations which breaks consistency
Keyword-style tag lists instead of natural descriptions

negativePrompts.note: Supports negative prompts. Keep to 15-25 terms maximum; more terms confuse the model.

strengths: Native 4K output resolution
Excellent text rendering for posters and UI mockups
Strong character consistency across generations
Responsive to photography terminology
tips: Structure prompts as: subject, action/pose, environment, style, technical details
Aim for 30-100 words; under 15 is too generic, over 150 causes conflicts
Use photography terms like lens type and lighting setup for realism
Describe spatial depth and layering explicitly for complex scenes
avoid: Prompts over 150 words which cause element conflicts
CFG guidance above 10 which leads to oversaturation and artifacts
Keyword-only prompts without natural sentence structure

tips: Start with the main subject and action, then layer setting and mood
Include text content in quotes if the image should contain readable text
Keep CFG guidance between 5.5 and 7.5 for best results
Describe modifications relative to the source image clearly
Focus on style transfer goals or specific element changes
Use depth descriptions to guide spatial modifications

negativePrompts.note: Full negative prompt support. Use terms like 'worst quality, low quality, blurry, watermark, bad anatomy' for best results.

strengths: Full negative prompt support for precise exclusion
Strong artist and art style recognition
Good natural language understanding at 8.1B parameters
tips: Write natural-language descriptions; keyword weighting syntax has no effect on SD 3.5
Leverage detailed negative prompts to exclude unwanted elements
Reference specific artists or art movements for reliable style matching
Keep CFG around 4.4 with moderate guidance for balanced output
avoid: Keyword weighting syntax like (term:1.3) which SD 3.5 ignores completely
BREAK or [[term]] tokens which have no effect on this version
Very high CFG values that cause oversaturation

strengths: Full negative prompt support for precise exclusion

negativePrompts.note: Recraft does not use a negative prompt field. Describe what you want positively and use style presets for control.

strengths: True SVG vector output, unique among generators
Accurate long text rendering including full paragraphs
Over 100 curated professional style presets
tips: Structure prompts as: image style, main content, detailed description, background, style details
Use the colors parameter with HEX codes for brand-accurate colors
Lower the artistic level setting for closer prompt adherence
Reference design movements like Swiss Style or Bauhaus for vector work
avoid: Expecting raster-only output; Recraft defaults to vector when appropriate
Overly vague style descriptions when precise presets are available
Ignoring the built-in style presets which outperform manual style prompting

negativePrompts.note: Negative prompts available on paid plans. Use them to exclude unwanted text or visual elements.

strengths: Best-in-class text rendering for logos, signs, and posters
Accurate multi-line typography in various styles
Magic Prompt feature for automatic prompt refinement
tips: Place text content in quotes for accurate rendering in the image
Specify typography style explicitly such as serif, sans-serif, or handwritten
Disable Magic Prompt when you need full manual control over output
Different style modes use different underlying models so choose carefully
avoid: Relying on Magic Prompt for precise technical compositions
Mixing too many text elements which reduces per-element accuracy
Ignoring the style mode selector which significantly affects output

negativePrompts.note: Supports negative prompts. Works similarly to Stable Diffusion negative prompt handling.

strengths: Handles prompts over 1000 characters with strong comprehension
Bilingual text rendering in Chinese and English
Large 80B parameter model with strong world knowledge
tips: Write long, detailed prompts; the model handles 1000+ characters well
Use scientific or geographic terms for accurate real-world depictions
Include bilingual text in quotes when rendering Chinese and English together
Specify camera and lens details for photorealistic output
avoid: Very short prompts that underutilize the model's long-context strength
Keyword-only prompts; use full natural sentences for best results
Expecting non-photorealistic styles without explicit art direction

tips: Be specific and descriptive in your prompt
Include details about subject, setting, lighting, and style
Use clear, unambiguous language to describe the desired output
avoid: Vague or overly brief prompts
Contradictory instructions in the same prompt
